details:
Multiple queue rows in planning/fall_of_nouraajd_issue_proposals.xlsx are marked Status=NOT_STARTED
but their described work is already implemented on main, so a controller that mechanically selects
them (they pass status+dependency eligibility) spends a full claim -> worker-investigation cycle only
to discover there is nothing to implement. Concrete examples verified against current source:

- [EPIC_02][STORY_03][SUBSTORY_04]Add CMapSessionStore (NOT_STARTED): class CMapSessionStore with
  get/put/evict/clear already exists (src/core/CGameContext.h:42-56, getMapSessionStore at :86,
  member at :112; CGameContext.cpp:104), and tests test_map_session_store_put_get_evict_and_ownership
  / test_game_context_owns_a_map_session_store already pass.
- [EPIC_02][STORY_04][SUBSTORY_03]Block movement cycles while transition pending (NOT_STARTED): the
  pending-transition guard already exists in CMap::move (src/core/CMap.cpp:578,
  sceneManager->isTransitionPending()), with test_map_move_blocks_new_turn_while_transition_pending
  already on main.
- [EPIC_03][STORY_05][SUBSTORY_05]Add save round-trip comparison harness (NOT_STARTED): reusable
  save/reload comparison helpers already exist in test.py (save_load_roundtrip:3048,
  assert_save_load_roundtrip:3112, snapshot_player_properties:538, plus
  test_save_state_summary_round_trips_through_save_load:15400).

These rows should be reconciled to a terminal state (DONE, or rescoped to the genuinely-remaining
delta) through a serialized workbook-only queue update so controllers do not waste claim cycles on
already-satisfied work. This is distinct from the existing open "eligible-but-prose-blocked" lead,
which concerns rows blocked by missing/unmerged prerequisites; here the rows are already complete.

evidence.recommendation: reconcile already-implemented NOT_STARTED rows to DONE or rescope via serialized workbook-only PR

fingerprint: queue rows not_started already implemented in source wasted claim
impact: Controllers spend claim+worker-investigation cycles on already-satisfied rows; queue drift hides true remaining P1 work
summary: Queue rows marked NOT_STARTED are already implemented on main, causing wasted claim cycles
